name: Sage White Beans
recipeIngredient: 2 cans of your favorite white beans
2 fresh winter savory or thyme sprigs
1 medium yellow onion, cut into 1/2 inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the beans into a saucepan with the bay leaf, savory, and two of the sage leaves. Warm gently until needed, allowing them to absorb the herb flavors. Remove the herbs and bay leaf, leaving the beans in their broth.
2. While the beans cook, chop the remaining sage leaves. Water-saute the onion with 1/2 teaspoon salt and a few pinches of pepper over medium heat until the onion begins to release its juices. Add the garlic and chopped sage; saute for about 10 minutes.
3. Add the wine and cook for a minute or two, until the pan is nearly dry.
4. Add the beans and their broth to the onions, along with 1/2 teaspoon salt and a few pinches of pepper. Lower the heat and cook for 20 minutes, adding water if needed to keep the beans saucy.
5. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Add parsley just before serving.
